summary: Dan Shapiro announces the Kickstarter launch of Robot Turtles, a board game designed to teach preschoolers programming concepts. Six days into the campaign, it had raised $188,000—about seven times his original goal. He emphasizes the game as a way for adults to meaningfully interact with kids through play. The post is essentially a call to action for readers to back the project and share it on social media before the print deadline of September 27.
key_points: Robot Turtles is a board game that teaches programming concepts to preschoolers through play
The Kickstarter campaign raised 7x its goal ($188,000) in just six days, demonstrating strong demand
Word-of-mouth and social sharing drove more traffic than any other channel
The game was positioned as a meaningful way for adults to interact with children
money_quotes: I'm humbled and amazed by the response – as of now, six days in, it's at $188,000, or about seven times what I'd hoped to raise.
I want to get this in the hands of as many eager grownups as I can, because having played it, it's just an amazing way to interact with kids.
More people have found it by email and social network referrals than anything else.
key_insight: A board game teaching preschoolers to program struck a massive nerve, raising 7x its Kickstarter goal in under a week largely through organic word-of-mouth sharing.
